root_text.an4.169:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo diṭṭheva dhamme sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:1.4: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo kāyassa bhedā sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:1.5: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo diṭṭheva dhamme asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:1.6: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo kāyassa bhedā asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo diṭṭheva dhamme sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti?
root_text.an4.169:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu asubhānupassī kāye viharati, āhāre paṭikūlasaññī, sabbaloke anabhiratisaññī, sabbasaṅkhāresu aniccānupassī.
root_text.an4.169:2.3: Maraṇasaññā kho panassa ajjhattaṁ sūpaṭṭhitā hoti.
root_text.an4.169:2.4: So imāni pañca sekhabalāni upanissāya viharati—
root_text.an4.169:2.8: So imesaṁ pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ adhimattattā diṭṭheva dhamme sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:2.9: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo diṭṭheva dhamme sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo kāyassa bhedā sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti?
root_text.an4.169:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu asubhānupassī kāye viharati, āhāre paṭikūlasaññī, sabbaloke anabhiratisaññī, sabbasaṅkhāresu aniccānupassī.
root_text.an4.169:3.3: Maraṇasaññā kho panassa ajjhattaṁ sūpaṭṭhitā hoti.
root_text.an4.169:3.4: So imāni pañca sekhabalāni upanissāya viharati—
root_text.an4.169:3.8: So imesaṁ pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ muduttā kāyassa bhedā sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:3.9: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo kāyassa bhedā sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo diṭṭheva dhamme asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti?
root_text.an4.169:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi …pe… paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ …pe… dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ …pe… tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ …pe… catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.169:4.3: So imāni pañca sekhabalāni upanissāya viharati—
root_text.an4.169:4.7: So imesaṁ pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ adhimattattā diṭṭheva dhamme asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:4.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo diṭṭheva dhamme asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo kāyassa bhedā asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti?
root_text.an4.169:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi …pe… paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ …pe… dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ …pe… tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ …pe… catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.169:5.3: So imāni pañca sekhabalāni upanissāya viharati—
root_text.an4.169:5.6: So imesaṁ pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ muduttā kāyassa bhedā asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:5.7: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo kāyassa bhedā asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.169:5.8: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.169:1.1: “Mendicants, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.169:1.3: One individual is fully extinguished in this very life by making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:1.4: One individual is fully extinguished when the body breaks up by making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:1.5: One individual is fully extinguished in this very life without making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:1.6: One individual is fully extinguished when the body breaks up without making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:2.1: And how is an individual fully extinguished in this very life by making extra effort?
translation_text.an4.169:2.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates observing the ugliness of the body, perceives the repulsiveness of food, perceives dissatisfaction with the whole world, observes the impermanence of all conditions,
translation_text.an4.169:2.3: and has well established the perception of their own death.
translation_text.an4.169:2.4: They rely on these five powers of a trainee:
translation_text.an4.169:2.5: faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom.
translation_text.an4.169:2.6: And these five faculties manifest in them strongly:
translation_text.an4.169:2.7: faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.an4.169:2.8: Because of the strength of the five faculties, they are fully extinguished in this very life by making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:2.9: That’s how an individual is fully extinguished in this very life by making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:3.1: How is an individual fully extinguished when the body breaks up by making extra effort?
translation_text.an4.169:3.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates observing the ugliness of the body, perceives the repulsiveness of food, perceives dissatisfaction with the whole world, observes the impermanence of all conditions,
translation_text.an4.169:3.3: and has well established the perception of their own death.
translation_text.an4.169:3.4: They rely on these five powers of a trainee:
translation_text.an4.169:3.5: faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom.
translation_text.an4.169:3.6: But these five faculties manifest in them weakly:
translation_text.an4.169:3.7: faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.an4.169:3.8: Because of the weakness of the five faculties, they are fully extinguished when the body breaks up by making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:3.9: That’s how an individual is fully extinguished when the body breaks up by making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:4.1: And how is an individual fully extinguished in this very life without making extra effort?
translation_text.an4.169:4.2: It’s when a mendicant … enters and remains in the first absorption … second absorption … third absorption … fourth absorption …
translation_text.an4.169:4.3: They rely on these five powers of a trainee:
translation_text.an4.169:4.4: faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom.
translation_text.an4.169:4.5: And these five faculties manifest in them strongly:
translation_text.an4.169:4.6: faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.an4.169:4.7: Because of the strength of the five faculties, they are fully extinguished in this very life without making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:4.8: That’s how an individual is fully extinguished in this very life without making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:5.1: And how is an individual fully extinguished when the body breaks up without making extra effort?
translation_text.an4.169:5.2: It’s when a mendicant … enters and remains in the first absorption … second absorption … third absorption … fourth absorption …
translation_text.an4.169:5.3: They rely on these five powers of a trainee:
translation_text.an4.169:5.4: faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom.
translation_text.an4.169:5.5: But these five faculties manifest in them weakly: faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.an4.169:5.6: Because of the weakness of the five faculties, they are fully extinguished when the body breaks up without making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:5.7: That’s how an individual is fully extinguished when the body breaks up without making extra effort.
translation_text.an4.169:5.8: These are the four individuals found in the world.”
